cxu_object.claim: When price spikes up ten dollars or more in two consecutive one-minute candles and the next candle stalls with a body less than thirty percent of the average spike candle body then auto-sell fifty percent of the long position to lock in gains at the top because momentum spikes followed by stalls indicate the move is exhausting

supporting_contexts.text: Spike detection on 1m candles: two consecutive candles with combined upward move >= $10, followed by a candle where body < 30% of the average spike candle body (momentum stall). This pattern indicates the spike is exhausting and price is likely to pull back. Selling 50% captures the top while keeping 50% for continuation.

Learned from missed opportunity: on 2026-04-17 a +$13.80 spike up occurred at 05:29 but the ntfy notification failed (encoding bug) and no auto-sell was in place. Price pulled back and the gains were lost. Auto-selling 50% on spike detection prevents this.

spikeThreshold.description: Minimum dollar move in 2 candles to count as spike

stallRatio.description: Max stall candle body as ratio of avg spike body

sellPct.description: Percentage of position to sell on spike top
